personality_voice.book_baseline: Adrian Pucey appears exclusively as a background Quidditch player in the book corpus. He is mentioned by name in commentary as a Slytherin Chaser — speeding toward goal posts, ducking Bludgers, dropping the Quaffle when distracted by the Snitch. No dialogue, no internal characterization, no personality traits are established beyond functional Quidditch participation.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: The film corpus is entirely empty. Adrian Pucey does not appear in any film scene provided.
personality_voice.difference: The character is completely absent from the films as represented in this corpus.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: A score of 0 is assigned not as a faithfulness judgment but because there is no film material to evaluate. The scoring framework cannot measure fidelity when one side of the comparison does not exist.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Pucey functions as a named Slytherin Chaser in the Quidditch match, serving a minor structural role as an opposing player. His agency is limited to in-game actions: advancing with the Quaffle, being blocked, dropping the Quaffle. He has no narrative agency beyond the match.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: Absent from the film corpus entirely.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No film material exists to score. Score is 0 by default due to absence, not due to a faithfulness failure that can be characterized.

motivations_internal_conflict.penalty_logic: Nothing to evaluate on either side. Score is 0 by default.

character_arc.penalty_logic: No arc exists in the books and no film material exists. Score is 0 by default due to absence of film material.

key_relationships.book_baseline: No meaningful relationships are established. Pucey is mentioned in proximity to other Slytherin players and as an opponent to Gryffindor Chasers, but no relational dynamic is characterized.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: The book corpus establishes no complexity or nuance for Pucey. He is a named background player referenced in match commentary only.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.penalty_logic: No film material to evaluate. The book characterization itself is minimal, so the absence of film material cannot be assessed as a loss of complexity.

score_caps_applied: No caps applied. Scores are 0 across all dimensions solely because the film corpus is empty — no film appearance exists to evaluate. This is an absence of data, not a scored faithfulness failure.
comparative.key_observations: Adrian Pucey does not appear in the film corpus at all. All six dimension scores are 0 by default due to the complete absence of film material, not because of any measurable faithfulness failure. The book corpus itself establishes only a minimal background character — a named Slytherin Chaser referenced in Quidditch commentary with no dialogue, personality, arc, or relationships. Because neither side of the comparison yields substantive characterization material, confidence is Low across all dimensions and globally. No meaningful FP evaluation is possible.
